strengths: 15+ years of fullstack experience with deep TypeScript/Node.js work at Optilyz, RIDE Capital, and re:cinq, well beyond the 6+ year requirement
Hands-on AI agent development at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks — a rare, explicitly required skill
Strong testing and craftsmanship background: TDD/pairing/trunk-based development at RIDE, author of test libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and testing workshop instructor
concerns: No Nest.js experience mentioned anywhere on the résumé, despite it being in the primary stack
Kotlin is absent and Java exposure is limited to older, non-web work (robotic arms at Altom, 2016-2018)
React appears in only one role (Optilyz); much of the frontend history is Ember.js, and Vitest/Playwright are not explicitly cited

key_factors.factor: Gaps in primary stack (Nest.js, Kotlin, limited React)

response.justification: Against the role's core competencies the candidate scores strongly: end-to-end fullstack delivery is evidenced by the Optilyz pricing platform (TypeScript, React, MongoDB, AWS CDK); AI agent development — often the hardest requirement to fill — is directly evidenced by MCP code generators and agentic tooling at re:cinq; cloud/containerization is covered by AWS CDK, Docker, and Kubernetes deployment of GISCollective; testing expertise is exceptional (TDD practice, authored test frameworks, taught testing courses); databases (MongoDB, MySQL, SQL) and Git are clearly present; and a CS degree plus polyglot history (TypeScript, D, Java, C++, Swift, PHP) satisfies qualifications. The deductions are for absent evidence on Nest.js and Kotlin, thin recent React/Vitest exposure, and no explicit auth/security work — learnable gaps for someone with this breadth, but real ones against the stated stack.
